SAP Portfolio Briefing | May 2026 · FY2026 |
Consumed $6.0M Budget $7.7M ACV $9.0M
AbbVie Inc
Life sciences and healthcare
Budget: $1.8M
Consumed: $1.4M
ACV: $1.9M
Account Insights
BTP Enterprise Agreement sits dormant across all four L1 domains while $1.9M ACV renews — convene an EA-led activation workshop within 30 days anchoring one GxP use case, or renewal becomes a shelfware negotiation.
Batch Release Hub is unactivated while Traceability Hub reversed in May, breaking the contracted end-to-end recall chain — request CTO sponsorship for a GxP architecture remediation sprint before FDA audit exposure compounds.
SAC BI consumes dashboards but lacks BTP grounding for Joule AI context, leaving AbbVie's $4.4M Data & AI budget funding analytics without the agentic intelligence the architecture was designed to deliver — anchor a planning use case by Q3.
Finance and Supply Chain both scale transactions faster than governance — SLP stalled, SSO dormant, Integration Suite unprovisioned — request CFO alignment on platform coherence as a renewal condition protecting the 70% budget headroom remaining.
Solution Areas
Solution Area
BAIP - Data & AI
48.4%
Budget Attainment
Budget$332K
Consumed$160K
ACV: $334K
EA Insights
AbbVie's Data & AI domain is architecturally inverted — SAC BI is consuming dashboards while BTP, its designed integration, extension, and AI grounding runtime, sits unprovisioned, leaving analytics floating without the data fabric or Joule context it was sold to deliver.
The dormant BTP tenant across both L2 areas means AbbVie has no sanctioned extensibility or AI-grounding layer for life-sciences workloads, forcing R&D, commercial, and supply analytics onto non-SAP platforms and fragmenting the regulated data context SAP designed BTP to centralise.
Without BTP activation, SAC BI cannot evolve into unified planning or agentic analytics — the architectural blueprint for trusted AI in pharma decision-making is broken at the foundation, undermining AbbVie's stated ambitions around AI-driven clinical and commercial insight.
Convene an EA-led Data & AI foundation workshop before December budget lapse — anchor a first BTP use case that grounds SAC BI in business context, or the renewal will default to a shrinking analytics-only conversation rather than a platform one.
Solution Area
BAIP - Technology Foundation
19.9%
Budget Attainment
Budget$547
Consumed$109
ACV: $405
EA Insights
AbbVie's entire Technology Foundation L1 is architecturally inert — BTP Enterprise Agreement, Integration Suite, and Build all sit dormant in unison, meaning there is no platform substrate for any SAP extension, integration, or agentic AI ambition across the enterprise.
The clean-core thesis is structurally broken at AbbVie — with BTP unprovisioned, every S/4HANA process extension and pharma-specific compliance customisation is being forced back into the core, accumulating upgrade debt that will surface at the next release cycle.
Build's dependency on BTP creates a compounding architectural dead-end — citizen-developer prototypes have no runtime, no integration fabric, and no Joule AI context, so the low-code extensibility story cannot industrialise even if a use case emerges.
Convene an EA-led Technology Foundation activation workshop within 30 days to anchor BTP, Integration Suite, and Build to a single named pharma use case — likely R&D-to-supply integration or GxP workflow extension — before renewal reframes this as shelfware.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
97.0%
Budget Attainment
Budget$1.1M
Consumed$1.1M
ACV: $1.3M
EA Insights
AbbVie's Finance and Spend Management architecture is operationally strong at the transaction edge — Concur and Ariba Buying are load-bearing — but the foundational SSO and BTP layers are dormant, leaving the domain without unified identity or extensibility scaffolding.
The zero-state of BTP Enterprise Agreement is the single most consequential architectural risk across this L1 — it simultaneously blocks SAC Planning's Joule AI activation, Ariba-to-S/4 clean-core extensions, and any agentic automation Finance contracted for.
Procurement and FP&A show the same architectural pattern — transactional layers scaling ahead of governance layers (SLP stalled, BTP dormant) — meaning AbbVie is executing spend and planning faster than it is controlling or intelligently augmenting them.
Convene an EA-led platform coherence workshop before Q3 renewal anchoring SSO activation, BTP enablement, and SLP governance as the three non-negotiable foundations — without them, AbbVie's Finance domain is a collection of point solutions, not an integrated architecture.
Solution Area
Supply Chain Management
32.1%
Budget Attainment
Budget$379K
Consumed$122K
ACV: $269K
EA Insights
AbbVie's Supply Chain GxP architecture is structurally incomplete — Batch Release Hub is unactivated while Traceability Hub runs in isolation, meaning the designed quality-decision-to-serialised-goods chain doesn't exist and the contracted end-to-end recall story cannot be executed.
The May reversal on Traceability Hub exposes an unresolved integration or master data break in the serialisation backbone, undermining the platform's role as the trusted source for regulatory track-and-trace before any multi-site, multi-ingredient lane can be onboarded.
Both L2 areas depend on Integration Suite and event-driven flows between quality, manufacturing, and network traceability — the absence of an activated batch release layer means there is no upstream event producer, leaving Traceability Hub architecturally orphaned.
Recommend an EA-led GxP architecture activation workshop immediately — sequencing Batch Release Hub kickoff, resolving the Traceability Hub data integrity break, and defining the integration contract between them is the only path to a defensible compliance posture.
Abbott Laboratories
Life sciences and healthcare
Budget: $1.8M
Consumed: $1.5M
ACV: $2.1M
Account Insights
Abbott's Business Data Cloud architecture is structurally incomplete—HANA Cloud operates as isolated database rather than federated fabric, leaving $2.1M ACV in Data & AI investment unable to deliver the AI-ready regulated data products life-sciences compliance requires; schedule EA activation workshop before renewal.
Technology Foundation shows Integration Suite as production backbone while Build sits dormant at pilot-only, meaning Abbott is scaling integration without extensibility governance—clean-core debt compounds with every workflow gap, threatening the BTP-led architecture already contracted; convene Q3 alignment workshop pairing capacity review with Build use-case activation.
Supply Chain's IBP runs S&OP in isolation without demand sensing or inventory optimization activated—Abbott operates a collaborative spreadsheet rather than AI-enabled planning platform, directly undermining pharma forecast accuracy and $1.5M consumed YTD value; lead architecture roadmap workshop before renewal to sequence module activation.
Finance and Spend Management shows Supplier Risk dormant behind active Ariba transactional modules—source-to-pay executes against un-vetted supplier master, an architectural omission incompatible with life-sciences compliance obligations; request CTO-sponsored risk-gate activation milestone for Q3 to close governance gap before Q4 renewal.
Solution Areas
Solution Area
BAIP - Data & AI
74.4%
Budget Attainment
Budget$89K
Consumed$66K
ACV: $113K
EA Insights
Abbott's Data & AI architecture is a visualization-only stack — SAC BI and Data Intelligence Cloud deliver insight pipelines, but the dormant planning layer breaks the insight-to-action loop SAP designed this domain to close.
HANA Cloud is deployed as a standalone database rather than the Business Data Cloud fabric anchoring Datasphere, SAC, and agentic AI — fracturing the federated data product blueprint that makes the rest of the L1 strategically defensible.
The common architectural fault across both L2s is a missing data fabric spine: without HANA Cloud federated to Data Intelligence Cloud and SAC, Abbott cannot deliver the AI-ready, regulated data products its life-sciences compliance and R&D mandates require.
Convene an EA-led Business Data Cloud activation workshop before renewal to re-anchor HANA Cloud as the fabric, add the planning module, and convert dormant contracted capacity into a coherent insight-to-action architecture.
Solution Area
BAIP - Technology Foundation
106.0%
Budget Attainment
Budget$129K
Consumed$137K
ACV: $135K
EA Insights
Abbott's Technology Foundation is architecturally inverted — Integration Suite runs as production backbone while Build sits dormant as a single-pilot tool, meaning integration scenarios are scaling without a corresponding extensibility layer to consume them cleanly.
The 'secure APIs for AI consumption' capability is structurally dormant despite Integration Suite being mission-critical, leaving Abbott with integration runtime but no API governance or AI agent enablement — blocking the AI-ready foundation Joule and agentic scenarios depend on.
Without Build wired into S/4 and Ariba process extensions, every workflow gap will be solved via core customization or external tools, compounding clean-core debt and undermining the BTP-led extensibility architecture Abbott has already contracted for.
Convene an EA-led Technology Foundation alignment workshop before Q3 true-up — pair an Integration Suite capacity and API governance review with a Build extension use-case session, so commercial expansion is anchored in a coherent platform roadmap, not runtime firefighting.
Solution Area
Customer Experience
59.8%
Budget Attainment
Budget$332K
Consumed$198K
ACV: $384K
EA Insights
Abbott's Customer Experience domain is architecturally two disconnected islands — C4C running as a departmental CRM and Incentive Management running as a payout engine — with no bridge between opportunity data and commission triggers, breaking the lead-to-cash-to-pay closed loop SAP designed.
The architecture is missing its middle layer entirely — no quota planning, territory design, CPQ, or service execution capability exists to translate C4C pipeline into Incentive Management payouts, leaving Abbott unable to govern seller behaviour as a connected system.
For a life sciences manufacturer where field force effectiveness and HCP engagement drive revenue, operating Customer Experience without service, field sales, or territory architecture means the domain cannot support Abbott's core commercial model — it is a finance-adjacent tool, not a CX platform.
Recommend an EA-led lead-to-cash architecture workshop before the next renewal cycle to redesign Customer Experience around connected personas and integration flows, because renewal defensibility now depends on architectural coherence, not incremental C4C or Incentive seat expansion.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
77.1%
Budget Attainment
Budget$670K
Consumed$516K
ACV: $883K
EA Insights
Abbott's Finance and Spend Management domain is architecturally bifurcated — FP&A planning and source-to-pay operate as isolated stacks with no evidence of SAC-to-Ariba data flows feeding procurement spend into driver-based planning, breaking the closed-loop finance narrative.
The source-to-pay spine is missing its risk gate — Supplier Risk dormancy means Sourcing and Buying execute against an un-vetted supplier master, an architectural omission that undermines compliance-driven life-sciences obligations Abbott cannot defer.
Both L2 areas show contracted capabilities sitting dormant behind active transactional modules — the common pattern is missing platform activation (BTP, Joule, Integration Suite) that would convert point tools into the unified intelligent-enterprise blueprint SAP designed.
Convene an EA-led Finance and Spend Management architecture review before renewal to re-baseline the integration map between SAC Planning, Ariba modules, and S/4HANA — the renewal will hinge on closing the platform-coherence gap, not feature expansion.
Solution Area
Supply Chain Management
100.0%
Budget Attainment
Budget$618K
Consumed$618K
ACV: $618K
EA Insights
Abbott's Supply Chain Management L1 architecture is single-pillar — IBP runs S&OP in isolation without demand sensing, inventory optimization, or response planning activated, leaving the unified planning blueprint structurally incomplete and commercially under-leveraged.
The absence of adjacent IBP modules signals a broken integration chain to execution systems — without supply response and inventory optimization, planning outputs cannot close the loop into S/4 or EWM, undermining Abbott's regulated-supply continuity objectives.
AI-powered demand prediction appears dormant despite being the core IBP architectural differentiator — Abbott is operating a collaborative spreadsheet, not an intelligent planning platform, which directly undermines pharma forecast-accuracy and service-level commitments.
Lead an EA-driven Supply Chain architecture roadmap workshop before renewal to sequence demand sensing, IO, and response planning activation — the renewal narrative must shift from planning-seat utility to unified, AI-enabled supply chain orchestration.
Cardinal Health Inc.
Wholesale distribution
Budget: $1.4M
Consumed: $1.0M
ACV: $1.7M
Account Insights
SAP Build is contracted at $52K but architecturally dormant — without a CoE-governed extensibility channel, every S/4HANA customization compounds clean-core debt; request an EA-led extensibility roadmap workshop before Q4 renewal.
Commerce Cloud operates as an isolated storefront with no BTP integration layer — the $168K CX investment cannot deliver omnichannel value until an architecture workshop sequences Service Cloud and Integration Suite activation.
Finance and Spend Management shows $612K contracted across Procurement, T&E, and Treasury but Contracts and Cash Management are dormant — the source-to-settle loop is broken; convene CFO-sponsored activation milestones for Q3.
70% of portfolio ACV ($1.2M) sits in solution areas with critical architectural gaps — request a CTO/CFO joint architecture alignment session to convert contracted value into operational capability before renewal negotiations.
Solution Areas
Solution Area
BAIP - Technology Foundation
68.4%
Budget Attainment
Budget$354
Consumed$242
ACV: $642
EA Insights
BAIP Technology Foundation rests on a single architectural pillar — SAP Build — and that pillar is contracted but not load-bearing, leaving Cardinal without a sanctioned extensibility fabric to keep S/4HANA clean-core at scale.
Without an embedded low-code practice, every workflow extension and custom app defaults back to core ABAP or shadow IT, fragmenting the technology foundation and compounding upgrade debt across every downstream LoB domain.
The absence of a governed citizen-developer operating model means BTP is positioned as a tools contract rather than a platform strategy — undermining Cardinal's ability to industrialize digital supply chain and pharma compliance extensions.
Convene an EA-led extensibility roadmap workshop to codify Build as the mandated extension channel, establish a CoE-governed use-case backlog, and align the technology foundation to clean-core principles before renewal.
Solution Area
Customer Experience
8.0%
Budget Attainment
Budget$360K
Consumed$29K
ACV: $659K
EA Insights
Cardinal Health's Customer Experience domain rests on a single Commerce Cloud pillar with no Service Cloud, Sales Cloud, or CDC signals — the architectural blueprint for unified customer engagement is structurally incomplete, leaving digital commerce disconnected from service and identity.
Commerce Cloud is deployed without adjacent order management orchestration or AI personalization layers — the contracted 'unified commerce' architecture is reduced to a transactional storefront, blocking the omnichannel trajectory healthcare distributors need to defend B2B share.
No BTP or Integration Suite footprint underpins this L1, meaning Commerce-to-S/4 order, pricing, and inventory flows are likely point-to-point — every future channel expansion will compound integration debt rather than leverage a composable platform.
Recommend an EA-led Customer Experience target-architecture workshop before renewal — the conversation must reframe Commerce Cloud as the anchor of a CX platform roadmap, not a storefront SKU, or renewal value will collapse.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
97.6%
Budget Attainment
Budget$1.0M
Consumed$1.0M
ACV: $1.1M
EA Insights
Cardinal's Finance and Spend Management architecture is operating as three disconnected islands — Procurement lacks supplier and contract foundations, T&E is saturated without procurement linkage, and Treasury is a single-capability outpost, breaking the source-to-settle closed loop SAP designed.
The Procurement stack is architecturally inverted — Buying and Sourcing run hot while Contracts and SLP, the governance and master-data foundations, are dormant, meaning negotiated value and supplier risk controls exist on paper but not in the execution layer.
Treasury is a one-capability deployment with Digital Payments live but Cash Management and Bank Account Management absent, leaving the payment factory disconnected from liquidity intelligence — a critical gap for a distribution business managing thin margins and high working-capital velocity.
Convene an EA-led Finance and Spend Management architecture alignment workshop before renewal to sequence SLP, Contracts, and Cash Management activation — the renewal narrative must shift from point-solution attainment to closing the documented end-to-end blueprint gaps.
Medtronic, Inc.
Life sciences and healthcare
Budget: $2.6M
Consumed: $2.1M
ACV: $3.2M
Account Insights
BTP Enterprise Agreement underpins four solution areas yet shows zero production activation — $3.2M ACV is funding architectural shelfware while clean-core extensions run in-core; request EA-led activation workshop within 30 days.
Finance domain contracted Watchlist Screening and Supplier Risk but neither is live — trade compliance and supplier governance have no architectural perimeter, exposing Medtronic to unscreened spend; escalate to CFO for Q3 activation mandate.
Commerce Cloud operates as a single-pillar CX architecture without Sales, Service, or CDP integration — the contracted omnichannel blueprint is structurally incomplete; convene architecture review to sequence one product-line storefront go-live.
Preferred Success advisory across Ariba and Concur is over-consumed while transactional platforms regress — $6.4M budget is funding enablement without architecture stabilization; tie renewal negotiation to activation milestones by Q4.

Solution Area
BAIP - Data & AI
0.4%
Budget Attainment
Budget$3K
Consumed$11
ACV: $0
EA Insights
Medtronic's entire BAIP - Data & AI domain rests on a single dormant pillar — BTP Enterprise Agreement — meaning the architectural blueprint for agentic AI, integration, and data has no live foundation, leaving every downstream SAP extension and AI grounding scenario architecturally stranded.
The convergence of dormant BDC Public and Database Cloud signals a deeper pattern: Medtronic contracted BTP as strategic platform but operates it as shelfware, so any S/4HANA or LoB customisation is almost certainly happening in-core, accumulating upgrade debt that contradicts clean-core principles.
For a medical device leader where regulated data integrity, supply traceability, and AI-driven R&D depend on a unified data and integration fabric, a dormant BTP foundation directly undermines Medtronic's ability to operationalise compliant AI and connected-device data flows.
Convene an EA-led architecture activation workshop within 30 days to anchor one production workload — Integration Suite for regulated data flows or AI Core for grounded clinical scenarios — converting contracted platform into the connective tissue the broader SAP estate requires.

BAIP - Technology Foundation
66.8%
Budget Attainment
Budget$123K
Consumed$82K
ACV: $159K
EA Insights
Medtronic's Technology Foundation is architecturally inverted — Integration Suite middleware runs in isolation while the BTP Enterprise Agreement underneath it sits dormant, meaning every new iFlow has no AI, data, or extension surface to land on.
The clean-core extensibility blueprint is broken at its foundation — Build is episodic, BTP is ungoverned, and Integration Suite is stalling, forcing custom logic back into the digital core and compounding technical debt before the next S/4HANA milestone.
There is no named platform owner across any L2 — agentic AI, low-code, and integration capabilities were contracted as one composable fabric but are being run as disconnected tools, undermining Medtronic's medical-device traceability and supply-chain agility objectives.
Convene an EA-led Technology Foundation activation workshop within 30 days to designate a platform owner and commit one anchor workload spanning BTP, Build, and Integration Suite — without it, the entire foundation layer becomes shelfware at renewal.
Solution Area
Customer Experience
27.9%
Budget Attainment
Budget$475K
Consumed$132K
ACV: $890K
EA Insights
Medtronic's Customer Experience domain is a single-pillar architecture — Commerce Cloud exists without Sales, Service, or Customer Data Platform companions, meaning the designed 360° customer engagement blueprint is structurally incomplete and cannot deliver unified omnichannel medical device journeys.
The B2B, B2C, and B2B2C channel architecture is contracted but only one storefront is live, breaking the unified commerce integration pattern with ERP, pricing, and order management that Commerce Cloud was designed to orchestrate across Medtronic's device portfolio.
Preferred Success advisory is architecturally decoupled from platform realization — success entitlement runs independently of Commerce Cloud activation, indicating no governance mechanism is translating architectural intent into integrated go-live execution against the original blueprint.
Convene an EA-led Customer Experience architecture workshop to sequence one product-line storefront go-live, define ERP and identity integration patterns, and establish whether the CX domain expands to Sales/Service or remains a Commerce-only footprint.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
91.6%
Budget Attainment
Budget$2.0M
Consumed$1.8M
ACV: $2.1M
EA Insights
Medtronic's Finance and Spend Management architecture is structurally incomplete at both ends — BTP is dormant for FP&A intelligence and Watchlist Screening is dormant for compliance control — meaning the domain has neither a predictive planning brain nor a trade-risk perimeter despite being contracted for both.
The source-to-pay blueprint is broken at its governance layer: Ariba Sourcing under-adoption combined with zero Supplier Risk activation means awarded spend bypasses Ariba Buying without risk-scored suppliers, collapsing the closed-loop sourcing-to-invoice architecture SAP designed.
Preferred Success entitlements across Ariba and Concur are over-consumed while the underlying transactional and risk platforms regress or stall — advisory capacity is being spent on enablement theatre rather than stabilizing the integration spine into finance.
Convene an EA-led domain architecture review before renewal to re-sequence BTP activation, Watchlist Screening wiring, and Supplier Risk integration as the three foundational control points — without them, Medtronic owns tools, not an architecture.

Industry Insights
BTP activation is universally dormant across AbbVie, Abbott, and Medtronic—none has moved beyond contracted shelfware, meaning GxP automation, regulatory traceability, and clean-core extensibility remain architectural promises rather than operational capabilities for this entire life sciences cohort.
Abbott leads the peer group on Integration Suite production deployment while AbbVie and Medtronic show zero provisioning—Abbott has operational middleware backbone but lacks the Build extensibility governance its peers have equally failed to activate.
Medtronic lags furthest on Finance compliance architecture—Watchlist Screening and Supplier Risk sit dormant against active Ariba transactions, while Abbott shows identical supplier governance gaps; AbbVie's SSO dormancy creates parallel audit exposure across all three.
Supply Chain planning maturity is weakest at Abbott—IBP runs isolated S&OP without demand sensing while Medtronic's contracted capabilities remain unactivated; neither matches the AI-enabled forecasting accuracy life sciences distribution and manufacturing demand cycles require.
Wholesale distribution
1 customer · ACV $1.7M
Budget vs Consumed by Customer
Cardinal Health Inc.
Budget: $1.4M
Consumed: $1.0M
ACV: $1.7M
Industry Insights
Cardinal Health stands alone in this single-customer cohort but benchmarks poorly against wholesale distribution peers — 70% of ACV tied to architecturally dormant capabilities means source-to-settle and omnichannel fulfillment lag industry leaders.
Cardinal Health's Finance and Spend Management shows contracted investment without operational activation — dormant Contracts and Cash Management modules break the procure-to-pay cycle critical for wholesale distribution margin optimization.
Cardinal Health's Commerce Cloud isolation represents a competitive risk — without BTP integration enabling real-time inventory visibility and order orchestration, the company cannot match omnichannel capabilities expected by healthcare distribution customers.
SAP Build dormancy at Cardinal Health signals extensibility debt accumulation — wholesale distributors requiring rapid customization for customer-specific pricing and logistics workflows will fall behind peers with governed clean-core strategies.
